# Eight Sessions or Eight Years?

> A structured narrative review of therapy duration for complex developmental trauma.

## Citation

Hogerzeil, S. J. (2026). Eight sessions or eight years? A structured narrative review of therapy duration for complex developmental trauma. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19171538

BibTeX:
@misc{hogerzeil2026duration,
  author = {Hogerzeil, Simon J.},
  title = {Eight Sessions or Eight Years? A Structured Narrative Review of Therapy Duration for Complex Developmental Trauma},
  year = {2026},
  doi = {10.5281/zenodo.19171538},
  url = {https://github.com/sjhogerzeil/narrative-review-therapy-duration},
  note = {ORCID: 0000-0001-6269-179X}
}

## Key metadata

- Population: Adults with complex developmental trauma (CPTSD, personality disorders, structural dissociation)
- Method: Critical interpretive synthesis (CIS; Dixon-Woods et al., 2006)
- Sources: 89 across 6 epistemological layers (academic, naturalistic, counterpoint, practitioner, client narratives, memoirs)
- Finding: Duration depends on endpoint definition — symptom reduction in weeks, structural change in years
- Framework: 4 factors — safety prerequisite, endpoint level, structural depth, elapsed time (sleeper effect)
- License: CC-BY-4.0
- AI disclosure: Conducted with substantial AI assistance (Claude Opus 4.6). See 2_methods/13_ai_methodology.md

## Key findings

1. The field's disagreement about therapy duration is an artefact of unstated endpoint definitions, not a genuine contradiction.
2. Five nested endpoint levels: symptom reduction (weeks), functional improvement (months), structural change (years), growth and reconnection (years to decades), recovery as ongoing direction (no endpoint).
3. The therapeutic clock starts at felt safety, not at the first session. Pre-safety therapy time inflates all duration estimates.
4. Nine independent theoretical traditions converge: structural change requires repeated relational cycles over developmental time and cannot be compressed by technique.
5. The strongest counterpoint evidence (EMDR intensive, MDMA-assisted therapy) measures symptom-level endpoints and does not contradict the long-duration thesis at the structural level.
6. Survivorship bias: all layers select for people who stayed in therapy. The 18–39% who drop out are invisible.

## Where to find things

- README.md — executive summary and navigation
- 4_discussion/1_synthesis.md — the central argument (63k words)
- 3_results/sources/ — 89 annotated source notes (one .md per source)
- 6_references/references.bib — complete BibTeX bibliography
- 2_methods/1_design.md — CIS methodology
- 2_methods/13_ai_methodology.md — AI disclosure (ICMJE 2026 compliant)
- CITATION.cff — machine-readable citation metadata
- _scripts/build_pdf.sh — build printable PDF
